corpus_note: Three Hebrew Strongs from disjoint corpus regions: early range H0001 (Genesis context, creation vocabulary), mid range H4397 (Psalms/prophetic context, messenger/angel domain), late range H8451 (Deuteronomy context, law/instruction vocabulary). Aramaic subscript case also included via H10001A which is Aramaic-range, demonstrating subscript_aramaic flag handling per Decision 11 edge case 2.

entries.definition: A male ancestor in the direct line; the founder or patriarch of a family, tribe, or nation; used figuratively of God as the originating source of creation and covenant relationship. Occurs in genealogical, covenantal, and honorific contexts throughout the Hebrew Bible.

A delegate or representative dispatched with an authoritative message; in the human domain denotes a royal or prophetic envoy; in the divine domain denotes a heavenly being sent on a divine mission. The term is semantically broad and context determines whether the referent is human or supernatural.

The body of divine instruction delivered through Moses; broadly any authoritative teaching or direction from God or a wise teacher. In postexilic usage the term narrows to the Pentateuch as a canonical unit, but in earlier texts it denotes specific priestly rulings or prophetic guidance. The term roots in the verb yarah, to shoot or to direct.

Aramaic cognate of Hebrew H0001. Occurs in the Aramaic sections of Daniel and Ezra in identical paternal and honorific senses. Carries subscript_aramaic flag to distinguish from Hebrew proper entries within the shared BriefLexEntry label space.
